qa_pairs.question: What happens to the Application Binary Interface (ABI) if only `-std=c++98` or `-std=c++11` is used without explicitly setting `_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI`?
qa_pairs.answer: If only `-std=c++98` or `-std=c++11` is used, the ABI does not differ; the compiler will use the default (i.e., newer) ABI.

qa_pairs.question: How does setting `_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI` affect the Application Binary Interface (ABI) used when compiling with `-std=c++98` or `-std=c++11`?
qa_pairs.answer: When `_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI` is set to `0` (for the old ABI) or `1` (for the new ABI) along with using `-std=c++98` or `-std=c++11`, it causes the specified old or new ABI to be used.

qa_pairs.question: Which compiler option is necessary to link to binaries compiled with the old Application Binary Interface (ABI) when using GCC v5.1 or higher?
qa_pairs.answer: To link to binaries compiled using the old ABI when using GCC v5.1 or higher, one must specify `_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI=0` when compiling the C++ code.
